answers.answer: B) Archival meant different things to each team - product likely expected data export + purge for compliance, while engineering interpreted it as soft-delete for potential reactivation. Reason: This is a classic terminology ambiguity problem. Archival has different meanings in different contexts - compliance archival (export then purge) vs operational archival (soft-delete for recovery). Solution: Define terms clearly in requirements phase, establish shared vocabulary.

Re-prioritized Requirements:

P0 (Must Ship - Security/Compliance/Core):
1. HIPAA audit log not recording deletions - Compliance/legal risk
2. Session timeout not enforced - Security vulnerability
3. Appointment double-booking bug - Core functionality broken, 3 clients affected
4. Timezone display incorrect for Hawaii/Alaska - Data accuracy issue

P1 (Should Ship - Important but deferrable):
5. PDF export crashes for 50+ pages - UX issue with workaround
6. Slow loading of monthly view for 500+ appointments - Performance, affects large clinics
7. SMS appointment reminders - High customer demand, business value
8. Bulk import from CSV - Improves onboarding efficiency

P2 (Nice to Have - Defer to next release):
9. Dark mode support - UX enhancement, non-core
10. Calendar sync with Google Calendar - Integration, deferrable
11. Patient self-scheduling portal - Large feature, 3 weeks insufficient
12. Multi-language support - Large engineering effort, needs translation/testing

Prioritization criteria: Security/compliance > core bugs > performance > new features. Consider customer impact, usage frequency, and implementation complexity given 3-week timeline with 4 engineers.
